text: The amygdala is more than just a fear button. It helps process threats and emotions, playing a key role in our survival. But what about joy, sadness, or even excitement? The amygdala is involved. Studies show it interacts with the prefrontal cortex to assess emotional responses. Understanding this can change how we perceive our own emotions. Curious about your brain? Hit follow for more insights!
